compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Plan-review PASS (signed) on iteration 8 — drove out genuine design issues each round: config placement (VerifyConfig->LLMConfig), a real correctness bug (fallback_strategy=None means wait_exponential per pydantic-ai source, NOT zero-wait — verified offline), the sync-safe teardown mechanism (asyncio.run(aclose)), and undeclared-dep concerns. Implemented + committed 4c5e03d89. Unified _build_retrying_anthropic_model covers both anthropic paths; SDK max_retries=0 + construction guard; LLMConfig.llm_retry_max_attempts(4)/llm_retry_max_wait_s(60); ADR 0037 stub; docs/config.md + regenerated env-vars.md. 13 transport tests + 170 affected tests green (incl env-registry drift gate); lint+typecheck clean. Comment-idempotency dropped as already-solved (comment_verdict run-marker + transport retry never re-executes tools). Advisory triage: ADR number 00XX->0037 concretized; spike extended to 500-no-header path; the remaining advisories (http_timeout forward-looking param, timeout-layer overlap) are reconciled by hoopoe/blackbear. Not closing until Verified +1.

compiled_state.description:
Own transient-failure retries at the httpx transport layer (`AsyncTenacityTransport`) so a mid-agentic-run blip self-heals below the agent graph, without re-executing already-run (side-effecting) tools. The transport-injection spike (GO, both paths) de-risks this load-bearing integration point.

## Why
A transient 429/529/5xx during a tool-using gate op must recover WITHOUT restarting the agent loop — a restart re-posts side-effecting tool calls and multiplies token cost. The community answer (pydantic-ai retries doc; Anthropic/OpenAI SDKs) is per-request retry at the HTTP transport, which re-sends only the one failed HTTP call while completed tool results are untouched. This is the riskiest integration point in the epic, verified by a spike (below) before the rest is built on it.

## What
### Dependency
Add the `retries` extra to the existing agents dep — spell it `pydantic-ai-slim[anthropic,retries]>=1,<2` (combined extras) in `pyproject.toml:108`. `[retries]` is a CONFIRMED extra (experiment log [1]) that ships `pydantic_ai.retries.AsyncTenacityTransport` + `wait_retry_after` and pulls in `tenacity` (confirmed installed at 9.1.4 by the spike). AC verifies `make install` provisions tenacity.

### The single unified Anthropic construction (covers ALL paths, not just loopback)
TODAY a custom `AnthropicProvider(base_url=_direct)` is built ONLY inside the loopback-proxy-bypass branch (`runner.py:233-240`, guarded by `self._model_override is None and resolved.startswith("anthropic")` AND a non-empty `_direct`); the normal model-string path lets pydantic-ai build its own provider internally (SDK default retries, no transport). Refactor to a SINGLE helper that, for any REAL anthropic model resolved on the normal or loopback path, builds the client below. The offline `model_override` test seam is a NON-anthropic FunctionModel and is untouched (it never enters this construction). So the unified helper covers BOTH real paths that today diverge (normal = SDK-managed provider, loopback = custom base_url); after this story both share one retrying client:
```
AnthropicModel(name, provider=AnthropicProvider(anthropic_client=AsyncAnthropic(
    base_url=<_direct or the SDK default>, max_retries=0,
    http_client=httpx.AsyncClient(transport=AsyncTenacityTransport(config=RetryConfig(...), wrapped=<default httpx transport>, validate_response=<callback>)))))
```
The loopback bypass just varies `base_url` (direct vs default); both paths get the SAME retrying client. `AnthropicProvider.__init__(api_key, base_url, anthropic_client, http_client)` confirmed (experiment log [4]); `AsyncAnthropic` accepts `max_retries` + `http_client` (log [5]).

### Retry config (proven by the spike)
- `RetryConfig(retry=retry_if_exception_type(httpx.HTTPStatusError) | retry_if_exception_type(httpx.TimeoutException) | retry_if_exception_type(httpx.NetworkError), wait=wait_retry_after(fallback_strategy=None, max_wait=<llm_retry_max_wait_s>), stop=stop_after_attempt(<llm_retry_max_attempts>), reraise=True)`.
- `wait_retry_after(fallback_strategy=None, max_wait=...)` — VERIFIED from the pydantic-ai SOURCE: when `fallback_strategy is None` the library sets it to `wait_exponential(multiplier=1, max=60)`. So the no-`Retry-After` path uses EXPONENTIAL BACKOFF (NOT zero wait / hammering); when a `Retry-After` header IS present it is honored (capped at `max_wait`). Proven offline: a 500-with-no-header→200 sequence retried with a ~1.05s inter-attempt wait.
- `validate_response(response)` callback turns retriable STATUS CODES into exceptions (they are not exceptions by default): `def validate_response(resp): if resp.status_code in {429, 529, 500, 502, 503, 504}: resp.raise_for_status()` — raising `httpx.HTTPStatusError`, which the `retry` predicate catches. **529 is included explicitly** (pydantic-ai's sample list omits it). Signature + behavior proven by the spike.
- `reraise=True` re-raises the last attempt's ORIGINAL exception on exhaustion (story mamba's classifier additionally unwraps a `tenacity.RetryError` defensively for the edge paths — complementary).

### Construction-time guard (no silent downgrade)
The transport-injection assertion fires at MODEL-CONSTRUCTION time inside `PydanticAIRunner.run()` (NOT process startup — the model is built lazily per run): assert the `AsyncAnthropic` client was built with `max_retries == 0` AND our retrying transport is present; if injection ever fails to apply, FAIL FAST rather than silently regress to the SDK default. The spike asserts `anthropic_client.max_retries == 0`.

### Config keys (added to LLMConfig — the runner's own config object)
Add `llm_retry_max_attempts: int = 4` and `llm_retry_max_wait_s: int = 60` to `LLMConfig` in `src/rebar/llm/config.py` — NOT VerifyConfig. Rationale (code-grounded): `PydanticAIRunner.run()` receives an `LLMConfig`, and every existing LLM OPERATIONAL knob (`timeout_s`, `max_tokens`, `max_iterations`) already lives on `LLMConfig` with a `REBAR_LLM_*` env fallback; `[verify]` holds verification-GATE semantic toggles, not model-runtime knobs. So add fields + `from_env` lines using `_llm_int(table, cli, "REBAR_LLM_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS", "llm_retry_max_attempts", 4)` and `_llm_int(table, cli, "REBAR_LLM_RETRY_MAX_WAIT_S", "llm_retry_max_wait_s", 60)`, read from the `[llm]` table. Threaded into the RetryConfig above at construction. Documented in `docs/config.md`.
- **Rollback / back-out:** setting `cfg.llm_retry_max_attempts = 1` (or the `REBAR_LLM_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS=1` env) is the operational back-out — it disables app-level retry without a code revert, restoring fail-fast behavior; the transport still installs but never retries.
- **Opt-out semantics (precise):** `cfg.llm_retry_max_attempts <= 1` makes `stop_after_attempt(1)` — the transport makes ONE attempt and does NOT retry. Combined with the always-on SDK `max_retries=0`, that is ZERO retries total: a deliberate FAIL-FAST. This does NOT restore the old undeliberate SDK 2-retry behavior (that inheritance is intentionally replaced by owned, config-controlled retry) — the toggle is "retry vs no-retry," not "new vs legacy."

### Retry observability
Each retry attempt (status/exception, attempt number, Retry-After wait) and final exhaustion log at WARNING (quiet by default), so a flaky provider window is visible without a debugger — via a `before_sleep` callback on the `RetryConfig` (VERIFIED: `RetryConfig(..., before_sleep=cb)` is accepted), which logs the attempt number + the wait it is about to sleep; exhaustion is logged where `reraise` surfaces.

### AsyncClient lifecycle
The `httpx.AsyncClient` holds a connection pool. Construct it per-run alongside the model (bounded to the run's lifetime) and ensure it does not leak across runs; if a shared/cached client is used, its shutdown is handled. AC asserts no unclosed-client resource warning across a run.

### Comment idempotency — NO new work (already solved)
`comment_verdict` (`src/rebar/llm/workflow/steps.py:260-284`) ALREADY embeds a `[rebar-run <run_id>/<step_id>]` marker and skips a duplicate within the same run. Because transport-level retry re-sends only the failed HTTP request BELOW the agent loop, completed tool calls (comments) are NEVER re-executed by a retry in the first place. So this story adds NO comment-tool change; it only ASSERTS (test) that a mid-run 429->200 self-heal posts no duplicate comment, which the existing marker + transport-level retry already guarantee.

### Timeout semantics + the hoopoe merge interface
The `httpx.Timeout` set on the shared `AsyncClient` is PER-ATTEMPT (httpx applies it to each individual request; the transport's retries wrap multiple such attempts). Story chief-contained-hoopoe contributes its `httpx.Timeout` by passing it into this story's construction helper — concrete interface: `_build_retrying_anthropic_model(name, *, base_url, cfg, http_timeout: httpx.Timeout | None = None)`; when hoopoe lands it supplies the object, and until then the helper uses a NON-None default `httpx.Timeout(cfg.timeout_s)` so there is ALWAYS a per-attempt bound (never an unbounded stall while waiting for hoopoe). One client, one timeout object, one transport — merged at this single owned seam. NOTE the pre-existing `model_settings['timeout']` (runner.py:285-286) ALSO derives from `cfg.timeout_s`; both carry the same value at different layers (SDK request timeout vs transport per-attempt read), a benign overlap that story hoopoe/blackbear reconcile when hoopoe's finer-grained `httpx.Timeout` lands.

### Streaming out of scope
The runner uses buffered `agent.run_sync()` (no streaming), so mid-stream partial-byte retry is not applicable; retries operate on whole buffered request/response pairs.

### ADR
The novel decision (retry at the httpx transport with SDK `max_retries=0`, justified by side-effect-safety — already-run tools are not repeated) is captured. To avoid a cross-story doc-ownership split, this story writes a short ADR stub at `docs/adr/00XX-transport-retry.md` (the `00XX` number is a placeholder — ADR numbers are being consumed concurrently in other worktrees, so blackbear, the sole ADR/docs owner, assigns the final number at integration); an AC covers the stub's presence.


### Notes on prior art, resources, and the fixed status set
- **Distinct from the reconciler's HTTP retry.** `_call_with_retry` (in the Jira/git reconciler) retries the RECONCILER's outbound HTTP at a different layer for a different peer; this story's transport retry is scoped to the LLM MODEL client only. They do not overlap or share config.
- **AsyncClient lifecycle (concrete).** The `httpx.AsyncClient` is created together with the `AnthropicModel` inside `PydanticAIRunner.run()` and is bounded to that run; it is explicitly closed at the end of the run by a `try/finally` that runs the async `aclose()` from the synchronous caller via `asyncio.run(http_client.aclose())` (VERIFIED offline: after `agent.run_sync()` has torn down its own internal loop, the stdlib `asyncio.run(http_client.aclose())` closes the pool cleanly with no error — no `anyio` dependency needed; the mechanism is best-effort — the `except` logs at WARNING (operator-visible, not silent) and never fails the run). A module-level cached client is deliberately NOT used — pass-1 runs finders in a ThreadPoolExecutor and each `run_sync` needs its own loop-bound client, so per-run construction is required for thread-safety. Asserted by a no-unclosed-client-warning test.
- **The retriable status set {429,529,500,502,503,504} is intentionally FIXED in code**, not a config surface — it is the well-known transient-HTTP set; exposing it as a knob would be scope creep with no operator need. `verify`/`llm` config governs only attempts + wait cap.

## Scope
- Dependency + the unified retrying-client construction (owned here; story chief-contained-hoopoe passes its `httpx.Timeout` INTO this same `httpx.AsyncClient` — this story is the named client-construction owner so the two settings merge on one client) + construction-time guard + the two config keys + docs + retry observability + client lifecycle + the spike.
- NOT in scope: message-history-replay resume (future Tier-2); streaming retry (no streaming); the per-tool/read TIMEOUT values (hoopoe); exit-code/gate posture (blackbear); any comment-tool change (already idempotent).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `pydantic-ai-slim[anthropic,retries]>=1,<2` in `pyproject.toml`; `make install` provisions `tenacity` (asserted).
- [ ] The spike integration test proves a `429`(+`Retry-After`)->`200` sequence is retried at the transport layer BELOW the SDK, honoring `Retry-After`, within one agent turn, on BOTH the normal and loopback-bypass base_url paths; AND a `500`(NO `Retry-After`)->`200` sequence is retried via the `fallback_strategy=None` default backoff (so the no-header path is verified, not merely asserted).
- [ ] The unified helper builds the retrying client for ANY anthropic model; SDK `max_retries=0` and transport-present are asserted by the construction-time guard (a test forces injection to fail and asserts FAIL-FAST, not silent downgrade).
- [ ] 529 + the transient set {429,529,500,502,503,504} + httpx timeout/network (NetworkError: connect/read/write) are retried; `Retry-After` honored and capped at `llm_retry_max_wait_s`.
- [ ] A mid-run transient (injected 429->200) self-heals and asserts NO duplicate comment (existing run-marker + transport retry).
- [ ] `llm_retry_max_attempts` / `llm_retry_max_wait_s` exist on `LLMConfig` (`src/rebar/llm/config.py`), are read from the `[llm]` table + `REBAR_LLM_RETRY_*` env, and default 4 / 60; a test sets `llm_retry_max_attempts=1` and asserts NO retry occurs (fail-fast opt-out), and the exhaustion path (all attempts fail) re-raises the original exception.
- [ ] The unified client always has a per-attempt `httpx.Timeout` (hoopoe's object when present, else the `httpx.Timeout(cfg.timeout_s)` default) — never unbounded; non-retriable HTTP statuses (400/401/403/404) are NOT retried by `validate_response` (asserted: single attempt, immediate surface).
- [ ] Each retry ATTEMPT is logged at WARNING on the `rebar.llm.runner` logger with the stable prefix `llm transport retry` (the `before_sleep` callback logs `llm transport retry: attempt <n> ... sleeping <s>s`), asserted via caplog matching that substring. EXHAUSTION (all attempts fail) is surfaced by the pre-existing generic `except` seam (`runner.py:353-365`) which already logs the failure — this story does NOT add a second exhaustion log with the retry prefix; the AsyncClient does not leak (no unclosed-client warning across a run); an ADR stub exists at the placeholder path WITH real content (Context / Decision / Consequences: the transport-retry-with-SDK-max_retries=0 decision and its side-effect-safety invariant), not an empty file.
- [ ] `docs/config.md` documents both keys; `docs/env-vars.md` is regenerated for `REBAR_LLM_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS`/`REBAR_LLM_RETRY_MAX_WAIT_S` (the CI drift gate passes); `test_config_llm.py`'s `_clean` fixture lists them.
- [ ] `make test`/`make lint`/`make typecheck` green.

Files: `pyproject.toml`, `src/rebar/llm/runner.py` (unified construction + guard + observability + per-run client teardown), `src/rebar/llm/config.py` (two LLMConfig keys + `from_env`), `docs/config.md`, `docs/adr/00XX-transport-retry.md` (stub), `tests/` (spike + config + guard + exhaustion + no-duplicate-comment).

## Verified library behavior (experiment log — pydantic-ai 1.107.0, tenacity 9.1.4, anthropic SDK, offline)
ACTUAL captured output (no billable call):
```
[1] '[retries]' in pydantic-ai-slim Provides-Extra: True          (the extra is real)
[2] wait_retry_after params: ['fallback_strategy', 'max_wait']     (standalone helper, honors Retry-After + caps)
[3] AsyncTenacityTransport params: ['config', 'wrapped', 'validate_response']
[4] AnthropicProvider.__init__ params: ['api_key', 'base_url', 'anthropic_client', 'http_client']
[5] AsyncAnthropic accepts max_retries + http_client: True         (SDK max_retries=0 + inject our client)
[6] tenacity installed: 9.1.4
```
SPIKE (the load-bearing proof; tmp/spike.py, re-run offline): an AsyncTenacityTransport in a custom
httpx.AsyncClient, forwarded through AnthropicProvider(anthropic_client=AsyncAnthropic(max_retries=0,
http_client=...)), retries a 429(+Retry-After)->200 sequence BELOW the SDK under agent.run_sync():
```
[normal path] requests=2 output='SPIKE_OK' -> PASS
[loopback-bypass path] requests=2 output='SPIKE_OK' -> PASS
SPIKE RESULT: GO — transport injection retries 429->200 below the SDK under run_sync on both paths
```

Additional VERIFIED facts (settle the `fallback_strategy=None` semantics — a code-blind reviewer
guessed it means zero-wait; the pydantic-ai SOURCE and an offline run prove otherwise):
```
pydantic_ai.retries.wait_retry_after source:
    if fallback_strategy is None:
        fallback_strategy = wait_exponential(multiplier=1, max=60)   # <- None => exponential backoff
Offline proof, 500 with NO Retry-After header, stop_after_attempt(4), reraise=True:
    500-no-Retry-After: requests=2 output='OK500' elapsed=1.05s   # retried, ~1s exp-backoff wait (NOT zero)
httpx.NetworkError is the base of ConnectError/ReadError/WriteError (transient transport set).
```

Retry-config + teardown API, VERIFIED offline:

RetryConfig(retry=..., wait=..., stop=..., reraise=True, before_sleep=cb)   -> accepted (observability hook)
asyncio.run(http_client.aclose())  after run_sync                            -> closes the pool cleanly, no error (stdlib; no anyio dep)
pydantic_ai.retries is the [retries]-extra public surface (AsyncTenacityTransport, RetryConfig, wait_retry_after)

```
```
These are pydantic-ai/httpx/tenacity/anthropic LIBRARY symbols, not rebar symbols — a code-grounding
pass restricted to the rebar snapshot must not read their repo-absence as non-existence (bug
succinct-formable-kite). The spike proves the architecture is sound BEFORE dependent stories build on it.

file_impact.reason: unified retrying Anthropic client construction + construction-time guard + retry observability + per-run client teardown
document the two retry config keys
spike (with+without Retry-After) + config + guard + exhaustion + no-duplicate-comment tests

compiled_state.title: Self-healing retry at the httpx transport layer (AsyncTenacityTransport), SDK max_retries=0
